description: Manually curated CVE variant chains from literature review. Each chain is a list of CVE IDs in chronological order (oldest first). An edge means the later CVE is related to the earlier one as a variant — including incomplete fixes, bypasses, regressions, same vulnerability class, different attack paths, or variant exploitation techniques.

chains.name: Spring4Shell (regression of 2010 fix)
chains.relationship: regression — same root cause class re-exposed

chains.name: ImageMagick ImageTragick variants
chains.relationship: incomplete fix — same input validation bypass

chains.name: sudo Baron Samedit
chains.relationship: related heap overflow variants in sudo

chains.name: Exchange ProxyLogon → ProxyShell
chains.relationship: different exploitation path for same SSRF surface

chains.name: Apache Struts OGNL injection variants
chains.relationship: same OGNL injection class, different entry points

chains.name: Spectre v1 → v2
chains.relationship: variant exploitation techniques for speculative execution
